title: rubus cordifolius subsp. scheutzii
intro: Rubus cordifolius subsp. scheutzii, also known as the Himalayan raspberry or mountain raspberry, is a subspecies of the raspberry plant native to the Himalayan region. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial activities. The plant is rich in vitamins, minerals, and other nutrients, providing various health benefits such as boosting the immune system, reducing oxidative stress, and supporting digestive health. Rubus cordifolius subsp. scheutzii can be prepared as herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, and its leaves, stems, and roots can be used separately or in combination to create various herbal preparations.
